bead.title: work loop: TTL/rotate the work-failed-routes audit list per bead
bead.description:
PROBLEM
applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion appends to a bead's work-failed-routes Extra list on every provider_connectivity failure. There is no TTL, no de-duplication, and no rotation. A bead that hits a flaky provider repeatedly over weeks accumulates dozens of identical entries; ddx-71629938 threads this list into Fizeau RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes, so every excess entry costs request-body size and routing-decision evaluation time. Beads that successfully run later still carry stale exclusion entries forever in their event history, with no signal that those providers may have recovered.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3843-3868 (applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion) only appends. cli/internal/bead/store.go has no schema-defined cap or rotation policy on the work-failed-routes field. The audit value is operationally useful for the next attempt's exclusion (per ddx-71629938) and for post-hoc forensics, but the two purposes have different lifetimes: exclusion needs a recent window (~1h), forensics wants persistence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define work-failed-routes as a bounded ring: max 32 entries, FIFO eviction when full. Newest entries always win.
- Define RouteExclusionWindow (already 1h in execute_bead_loop.go) as authoritative for what's threaded into Fizeau RouteRequest: only entries whose At timestamp is within the window appear in ExcludedRoutes. Older entries stay in the audit list (up to the 32-entry cap) but do not affect routing.
- De-duplicate on insert by (provider, model): if an identical (provider, model) already exists in the list, update its At timestamp and bump its count rather than appending.
- Add a count field per entry so operators can see "this provider has failed 7 times in the last hour" at a glance.
- Migrate existing beads on first read: on Store.Get, if work-failed-routes has duplicates or >32 entries, collapse silently.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change RouteExclusionWindow's value.
- Do not garbage-collect across beads (per-bead audit stays).
- Do not move the audit to a separate file.

DEPS
ddx-71629938 (landed) — the consumer that reads work-failed-routes.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestFailedRoutes_DeduplicatesOnSameProviderModel asserts that two consecutive applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls with the same (provider, model) result in ONE entry whose At timestamp is the second call's and whose count is 2.
2. TestFailedRoutes_CapsAt32Entries asserts that after 33 distinct (provider, model) failures, the list contains exactly 32 entries and the OLDEST has been evicted (FIFO).
3. TestFailedRoutes_ExclusionWindowFiltersOldEntriesFromRouteRequest asserts that entries older than RouteExclusionWindow are present in the bead's Extra audit list but absent from the Fizeau RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes payload.
4. TestFailedRoutes_StoreGetCollapsesLegacyDuplicates asserts that a bead loaded from .ddx/beads.jsonl with pre-migration duplicate entries is normalized on read (transparent migration).
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
